message:
feat(abi): nativize DNS resolver and f128 parse internals, fix stdio type safety

Replace dlsym_passthrough! with native Rust implementations for the DNS
resolver family (__res_mkquery, __res_nclose, __res_ninit, __res_nmkquery,
__res_nquery, __res_nquerydomain, __res_nsearch, __res_nsend,
__res_querydomain, __res_send, __res_state) and the f128 parse variants
(__strtof128_internal, __wcstof128_internal). This eliminates 12 GCT
symbols, bringing them under full frankenlibc control.

Additionally:
- stdio_abi: change stdin/stdout/stderr statics from `usize` to
  `*mut c_void` for correct C ABI type, and expand _IO_2_1_* aliases
  from single-usize to 256-byte mutable buffers to accommodate glibc
  _IO_FILE_plus struct layout without segfault.
- unistd_abi: widen RESOLV_CONFIG visibility to pub(crate) so
  glibc_internal_abi DNS implementations can reference it.
- wchar_abi: update getwchar/putwchar/wscanf/vwscanf to use the new
  typed stdin/stdout pointers directly instead of casting from usize.
- stdlib_abi_test: add Mutex serialization for ecvt/fcvt tests that
  share a process-wide static buffer, preventing data races under
  parallel cargo test.
- glibc_internal_abi_test: add 10 new tests covering __res_mkquery
  (valid, unsupported op, buffer too small), __res_send (null msg),
  __res_state (non-null + thread stability), __strtof128_internal
  (parse + empty), and __wcstof128_internal (parse).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
